title: Garh Panchkot
tagline: Garh Panchkot — heritage city of West Bengal

overview.short: Garh Panchkot, meaning "Fort of the Five Clans", is a ruined fort located in the eastern part of India at the foothills of Panchet Hill in the district of Purulia, West Bengal.
overview.description: Garh Panchkot, meaning "Fort of the Five Clans", is a ruined fort located in the eastern part of India at the foothills of Panchet Hill in the district of Purulia, West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Gobag
topPlaces.description: Gobag is a village in the Neturia CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Panchet Hill
topPlaces.description: Panchet Hill, referred to in Bengali as Panchakot Pahar, is located in Neturia (community development block) at the north-eastern end of Purulia district, in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Harmadih
topPlaces.description: Harmadih (also spelled Harmadi) is a village in the Neturia CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Ramchandrapur, Purulia
topPlaces.description: Ramchandrapur is a village in Santuri CD Block in Raghunathpur subdivision of Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Muraddi
topPlaces.description: Muradi (muraddi) is a village in the Santuri CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India. Muradi Dam is a tourist spot.

topPlaces.name: Murulia
topPlaces.description: Murulia is a census town in the Santuri CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Sarbari
topPlaces.description: Sarbari is a village in West Bengal, India. It is located within Neturia CD block of Raghunathpur subdivision of Purulia district.

topPlaces.name: Baranti
topPlaces.description: Baranti is a small tribal village in the Santuri (community development block) in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is situated beside Baranti Lake.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Garh Panchkot?
faq.a: The best time to visit Garh Panchkot is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Garh Panchkot?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport, Ranchi (151 km) and the nearest railway station is Dhanbad Junction (41 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Garh Panchkot?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Garh Panchkot at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Garh Panchkot?
faq.a: Stays in Garh Panchkot start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Garh Panchkot?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gobag, Panchet Hill, Harmadih, Ramchandrapur, Purulia, Muraddi.
